features.title: Next.js app scaffold under src/ with Tailwind, gates wired
features.description: Scaffold the Next.js App Router app under src/ (src/app, src/components, src/lib) with Tailwind CSS, a dark-theme base layout with navbar, and a dashboard placeholder page; wire typecheck/lint/test/build npm scripts so verify.sh product mode (triggered by src/ existing) passes. README pins Next.js 16 + React 19 + Tailwind — re-verify current stable versions per P1 before installing, and the engine ts-node scripts must keep working under any tsconfig changes.
acceptance: npm run build completes a production Next.js build (exit 0)
npm run test runs Vitest and at least one smoke test passes (product mode requires a test script)
npm run lint statically analyzes src/ in addition to scripts/ (lint script visibly includes src)
Dashboard page at / renders the app shell (navbar + AnswerFlow branding), asserted by a Vitest render test until Playwright lands
bash scripts/verify.sh exits 0 in product mode, with engine meta-gates (ts-node state scripts, biome, shellcheck) still green

features.title: Prisma + SQLite schema (core models) and seed fixtures
features.description: Add Prisma ORM with an embedded SQLite database: core models User, Project, Question, Answer, Source, Chunk with relations and status fields, plus a `seed` npm script that pushes the schema and populates realistic fixtures (SSO/backup/GDPR knowledge sources sliced into chunks, one sample questionnaire project with questions including a few near-duplicates, and teammates). The gate's E2E seed shim (scripts/seed.ts) delegates to `npm run seed`, so that script name is a contract.
acceptance: npm run seed exits 0 on a fresh clone (creates the SQLite db, pushes schema, inserts fixtures) and is idempotent on a second run (no duplicates or constraint crashes)
A Vitest integration test reads seeded data through the shared Prisma client: at least 1 project, 8 questions, 2 sources, 10 chunks
The SQLite db file and generated client artifacts are gitignored (clean git status after seeding)
bash scripts/verify.sh exits 0

features.title: Playwright E2E harness wired into verify --e2e
features.description: Configure Playwright with a webServer that boots the Next.js app against the seeded SQLite database, and define the `e2e` npm script that verify.sh --e2e invokes after the seed shim. Include a smoke spec proving the loop end to end: the dashboard loads and shows the seeded project.
acceptance: npm run e2e runs headless Chromium and the smoke spec passes: dashboard lists the seeded project name
A deliberately failing expectation makes npm run e2e exit non-zero (shown once in PR evidence, then removed)
bash scripts/verify.sh --e2e exits 0 end to end (seed step + e2e step both green)

features.title: Staging deploy: production serve, health check, deploy config + operator guide
features.description: Make the app deployable so the operator gets a staging link: Next.js standalone production output, an /api/health endpoint, Dockerfile + Fly.io config with a persistent volume for the SQLite file (provider assumed Fly.io pending the operator's answer in QUESTIONS.md), and a plain-English click-by-click deploy guide. Agents do not execute the deploy — the guide is the deliverable; everything else is verified locally.
acceptance: npm run build then npm run start serves the production build; GET /api/health returns 200 with {"status":"ok"} (integration or E2E test)
Dockerfile builds from the standalone output and fly.toml mounts a persistent volume at the SQLite path (config contents asserted by a test or the evaluator)
docs/DEPLOY.md exists with click-by-click operator steps in 8th-grade plain English
bash scripts/verify.sh exits 0

features.title: Project creation + questionnaire upload/parse API (CSV/XLSX)
features.description: API plus minimal UI to create a project and upload a questionnaire spreadsheet: parse .csv and .xlsx server-side (read-excel-file or equivalent — verify library health per P1) and return detected headers plus preview rows for the mapping step. Raw rows are held for mapping; Question rows are not created yet (that is F-0006).
acceptance: POST /api/projects creates a project and the dashboard New Project flow reaches an upload screen (E2E)
Uploading an .xlsx fixture returns headers plus preview rows as JSON (integration test with committed fixture)
Uploading a .csv fixture with quoted commas and embedded newlines parses correctly (integration test)
Unsupported file types and files over 10 MB get a 4xx with a plain-English message (integration test)
bash scripts/verify.sh --e2e exits 0

features.title: Column Mapper UI persisting questions
features.description: After upload, an interactive Column Mapper lets the user pick which spreadsheet columns hold question text, category, and section, with auto-suggested defaults and a live preview; confirming creates the project's Question rows in original order. Spec: Smart Column Mapping.
acceptance: E2E: create project, upload the CSV fixture, map columns, confirm — the project's questions API then returns the expected questions in row order
Auto-suggestion picks the most question-like column on the fixture (unit test of the heuristic)
Category column is optional: skipping it imports questions with null category (test)
Re-importing into the same project either replaces or appends per an explicit user choice, and a test covers the chosen semantics
bash scripts/verify.sh --e2e exits 0

features.title: Review workspace: question list with status, search, filters
features.description: The project workspace page: lists the project's questions with status chips (unanswered/drafted/approved), full-text search, category filter, and a detail panel showing the question and its current answer. Must work against seeded data alone so it can ship before intake completes.
acceptance: E2E: opening the seeded project's workspace renders every seeded question with the correct status chip
E2E: text search and category filter narrow the visible list
E2E: selecting a question opens a detail panel with the question text and its current answer or an explicit empty state
bash scripts/verify.sh --e2e exits 0

features.title: Local RAG engine: BM25 + TF-IDF cosine + synonym expansion
features.description: Implement src/lib/rag.ts: BM25 keyword scoring combined with TF-IDF cosine similarity over knowledge chunks, with synonym-boosted query expansion (e.g. SSO to Okta/SAML/OAuth, backup to recovery/snapshots/DR). Deterministic pure-function core with a calibration suite over the seeded corpus.
acceptance: search(query, k) returns top-k chunks with stable deterministic ranking on a fixed fixture corpus (unit tests)
Synonym expansion works: the query SSO retrieves a chunk containing only SAML (unit test)
Empty, stopword-only, and no-match queries return an empty result without throwing (unit tests)
Calibration: at least 5 canonical queries each retrieve their expected seeded chunk within the top 3 results, and the test fails on regression
bash scripts/verify.sh exits 0

features.title: Draft answers from the knowledge base with citations
features.description: Per-question Draft Answer (and project-level Draft All): retrieve top chunks via the RAG engine, compose an extractive draft with numbered citations bound to real chunk IDs, and persist it as the question's answer with status drafted; the workspace renders the draft with clickable citations revealing the source passage and document name. Questions with no usable retrieval get an explicit needs-info state instead of a fabricated answer.
acceptance: POST /api/questions/:id/draft persists an answer whose every citation references an existing chunk ID (integration test on seed data)
No-hit questions produce a needs-info result with zero citations — never invented text (unit test with an empty corpus)
E2E: clicking Draft on a seeded question renders a draft with at least one citation; clicking the citation shows the source chunk text and document name
E2E: Draft All moves every unanswered question to drafted or needs-info
bash scripts/verify.sh --e2e exits 0

features.title: Tone profiles: six answer-formatting personas
features.description: Apply one of six communication profiles (Concise, Detailed, Yes/No, Formal, Security, Plain) when composing drafts, selectable as a per-project setting. A pure formatting layer over the extractive draft so each profile is snapshot-testable.
acceptance: The same retrieval input formatted by each of the six profiles yields distinct outputs matching the spec shape (Concise max 2 sentences; Detailed bullet breakdown; Yes/No leads with Yes or No; Formal procurement language; Security policy-verification format; Plain simplified vocabulary) — unit or snapshot tests per profile
The per-project profile setting persists and is honored on the next draft (integration test)
E2E: changing the profile and re-drafting changes the rendered answer shape
bash scripts/verify.sh --e2e exits 0

features.title: KB document parsing + chunking pipeline (.txt/.pdf/.docx)
features.description: Upload API for knowledge-base sources: extract text (.txt native, .pdf via pdf-parse, .docx via mammoth — verify library health per P1), slice into deterministic overlapping chunks with source and position metadata, and index them for RAG retrieval. Fixture-tested with one real committed file of each type.
acceptance: Uploading each fixture type (.txt, .pdf, .docx) creates a Source plus chunks whose text matches the fixture content (integration tests)

Chunking is deterministic and parameterized (target size + overlap), does not split mid-word, and preserves chunk order — unit tests including a short doc yielding one chunk and an empty doc rejected with 4xx
RAG search finds content from a newly uploaded document immediately after ingest (integration test)
A corrupt or password-protected PDF returns a 4xx plain-English error without crashing (test)
bash scripts/verify.sh exits 0

features.title: Sources management UI
features.description: The /sources page: upload knowledge documents through the browser, list them with name, type, chunk count, and upload date, inspect a source's chunks, and delete a source (cascading its chunks). Completes the operator-visible loop for the KB pipeline.
acceptance: E2E: uploading a .txt through the page shows it in the list with name, type, chunk count, and date
E2E: deleting a source removes it from the list and subsequent drafting/search no longer cites it
E2E: the empty state shows plain-English guidance
bash scripts/verify.sh --e2e exits 0

features.title: Teammates directory + question assignment
features.description: Team page to add teammates (name + email records — local-first, no authentication in this phase) and a workspace control to assign a question owner, with an assignee filter. Real login/auth is explicitly out of scope until the operator asks for it.
acceptance: E2E: the team page adds a teammate and lists the directory
E2E: assigning an owner from the question detail persists across reload and shows the owner on the question row
E2E: filtering the workspace by assignee narrows the list
Evaluator check: no authentication surface (login, sessions, passwords) was added
bash scripts/verify.sh --e2e exits 0

features.title: Comment threads on questions
features.description: Context threads directly on questions: post comments as a selected teammate, render author and timestamp, and show a comment-count badge on the question list. Persistence via the existing Prisma models plus a Comment model migration.
acceptance: E2E: posting a comment as a chosen teammate renders it in the thread with author name and timestamp
E2E: comments persist across reload, ordered oldest first
E2E: the question list row shows a comment-count badge that updates
bash scripts/verify.sh --e2e exits 0

features.title: Answer approval workflow
features.description: Approve and unapprove drafted answers with status transitions unanswered/drafted/approved, a project progress meter, and edit-reverts-approval semantics (editing an approved answer drops it back to drafted, per the salvage-review flywheel rules).
acceptance: E2E: the Approve action moves a drafted answer to approved and updates the status chip
Editing an approved answer reverts its status to drafted (unit + E2E)
E2E: the project progress meter (approved/total) updates after approval
bash scripts/verify.sh --e2e exits 0

features.title: Similarity clustering engine
features.description: Group near-duplicate questions per project using cosine similarity over question text with a tunable threshold, exposed via a clusters API. Reuses the RAG vector primitives; deterministic unit tests on fixtures with known duplicate groups.
acceptance: A fixture of 12 questions containing 3 known near-duplicate groups clusters into exactly those groups (unit test)
Distinct questions remain singletons at the default threshold (unit test)
GET /api/projects/:id/clusters returns cluster groups with member question IDs (integration test on seed data, which includes near-duplicates)
bash scripts/verify.sh exits 0

features.title: Cluster UI: propagate answer + bulk approve
features.description: Clusters view in the workspace showing grouped near-duplicate questions with one-click Propagate Answer (copy a chosen answer to all members) and Bulk Approve (approve all answered members at once). Completes the duplicate-resolution workflow from the spec.
acceptance: E2E: the clusters view groups the seeded near-duplicate questions
E2E: Propagate Answer copies the source answer to every member question
E2E: Bulk Approve sets every answered member to approved in one action
bash scripts/verify.sh --e2e exits 0

features.title: CSV + JSON export
features.description: Export a project's completed questionnaire as CSV (question, answer, status, category columns) and structured JSON (including citations metadata), downloadable from the workspace. Server-generated with content-type and filename headers.
acceptance: The CSV export contains one row per question with question, answer, status, and category matching database state (test parses the downloaded CSV)
The JSON export includes citations metadata and validates against an asserted schema (test)
E2E: the workspace export menu downloads both formats with correct content types
bash scripts/verify.sh --e2e exits 0

features.title: XLSX + DOCX export
features.description: Export the questionnaire as a formatted Excel sheet (write-excel-file) and a styled Word document (docx library) — verify both libraries' health per P1 at brief time. Note: this is generic export; original-format fill-in of the customer's own file is a separate Later feature.
acceptance: The XLSX export round-trips through read-excel-file in a test: all question/answer rows present with headers
The DOCX export contains each question as a heading with its answer paragraph, asserted by extracting text via mammoth in a test
E2E: both downloads succeed with correct content types and non-zero size
bash scripts/verify.sh --e2e exits 0
